title: Generate requirements for a compact air purifier for a bedro
stakeholder_needs: Generate requirements for a compact air purifier for a bedroom. It should cover at least 25 square meters, operate below 35 dB in sleep mode, and comply with EU safety and environmental regulations.
analysis: Key stakeholders: (1) Primary user (bedroom occupant): needs effective purification for allergy/odor/dust in ~25 m², very low noise during sleep, simple controls, minimal light, low maintenance, acceptable size/appearance. (2) Household/secondary users: safety around children/pets, ease of moving, intuitive setup, filter availability and cost. (3) EU regulators/compliance: product safety (LVD if mains, EMC), chemical restrictions (REACH), RoHS, WEEE, Ecodesign/energy labeling where applicable, packaging waste, CE marking and technical documentation. (4) Manufacturer/service: cost targets, manufacturability, supply chain for filters/fan/motor, reliability/warranty, testability (CADR, noise, EMC), after-sales support. Derived requirement themes: performance (coverage via CADR/ACH), acoustics (≤35 dB(A) sleep), safety (electrical, mechanical, thermal, fire), environmental (materials, end-of-life, energy), usability (sleep mode behavior), and verification methods (standardized tests). Missing context likely affects sizing targets, CADR setpoint, filtration grade (HEPA), VOC/odor needs, and whether mains/battery.
clarification_questions: What target pollutants must it address (e.g., PM2.5/allergens only, or also VOCs/odors via activated carbon), and do you require a specific filtration grade (e.g., HEPA H13/H14)?
What room assumptions should define “covers 25 m²” (ceiling height, target air changes per hour, and whether performance should be expressed as CADR to a specific standard such as AHAM/EN/ISO)?
Any constraints on form factor and power (max dimensions/weight, mains only vs. battery, and target energy use/efficiency)?

table.title: DFMEA - Compact Bedroom Air Purifier (EU)
rows: Provide portable backup power without thermal event
Fire, smoke inhalation risk, property damage, regulatory noncompliance, recall
Internal short due to separator defect; overcharge; mechanical crush; high ambient temp
Cell supplier qualification; pack mechanical protection; BMS OVP/UVP/OCP; cell spacing; UL1642/IEC62133 compliant cells
BMS firmware tests; end-of-line pack test; thermal abuse test; incoming cell inspection AQL
Add redundant temperature sensors and thermal fuse; add flame-retardant barrier; improve vent path; tighten cell supplier PPAP and lot traceability
Design FMEA review scheduled; BMS requirements drafted; supplier audit planned
Add pack crush test early; ensure venting not blocked by housing ribs
If product is mains-only variant, mark as not applicable for that SKU and remove pack from BOM
Provide airflow to meet CADR and coverage requirement
No purification; user dissatisfaction; potential overheating of windings
Bearing seizure; debris ingestion; undervoltage; driver MOSFET failure; firmware lockup
Motor derating; protective grille; current limiting; conformal coating on PCB; FW watchdog
EOL functional run test; tach feedback monitoring; HALT for motor life
Add stall detection with shutdown; increase bearing spec (L10); add debris screen; improve driver thermal design
Tach feedback available in prototype; screening concept in CAD
Include debris ingestion test with pet hair and dust loading
Stall handling must avoid audible alarms in sleep mode
Draw room air into unit with low turbulence and low noise
Reduced CADR; increased noise; motor overload
User places unit against wall/curtain; small inlet area; clogged prefilter
Inlet geometry with clearance guides; anti-tip feet spacing; prefilter access prompts
User manual guidance; airflow sensor (current vs RPM) plausibility; noise test at max load
Add 360° inlet or side inlets; add physical standoff ribs; implement filter clog indicator using ΔP or current map
CAD iteration started for side inlet; indicator algorithm in backlog
Design for real bedroom placement patterns; validate with curtain proximity tests
Ensure child finger probe protection per EN 60335 access rules
Remove PM2.5/allergens to target efficiency and CADR
Filter media bypass due to poor fit
Particles pass through; allergy symptoms persist; complaint/returns
Tolerance stack-up; warped frame; missing gasket; user installs incorrectly
Keyed filter design; gasket compression spec; assembly poka-yoke; incoming filter frame inspection
Leak test sampling; CADR verification; visual fit check; user guidance in app/label
Add 360° continuous gasket; add latch to enforce compression; introduce EOL leak test (aerosol) for 100% or higher sampling
Gasket supplier identified; latch concept modeled
Add installation detection switch; consider RFID filter to ensure correct part
Avoid increased pressure drop that would raise noise >35 dB(A) in sleep
Carbon saturation or low adsorption capacity
Low carbon mass; poor pellet quality; high VOC load; long replacement interval
Specify iodine number and bed mass; supplier COA; replacement interval label
User feedback; odor panel tests; optional VOC sensor trend; maintenance reminder
Increase carbon mass or use impregnated carbon; add VOC sensor-based replacement prompt; qualify second source
COA template drafted; VOC sensor evaluation planned
Odor performance must be substantiated; avoid misleading claims under EU rules
Carbon dust must not shed into airflow
Provide structural integrity, aesthetics, and safe enclosure
Sharp edges; ingress to live parts; rattle noise; reduced durability
Drop impact; UV aging; weak ribs; poor material selection
Use UL94 V-0/V-2 as needed; drop test spec; rib design rules; UV-stabilized resin
Incoming resin verification; drop test; visual inspection; dimensional checks
Increase wall thickness at stress points; add impact ribs; add corner radii; validate with 1 m drop all orientations
Drop test plan created; FEA started
Design for WEEE recyclability; avoid mixed materials where possible
Minimize light leakage from UI for bedroom use
Convert mains to safe low-voltage power; meet LVD/EMC
Damage to electronics; potential overheating; safety hazard
Feedback loop failure; component short; surge event
Use certified PSU (EN 61558/EN 62368 as applicable); OVP crowbar; surge protection MOV/TVS
Supplier safety certificates review; HiPot test; surge/ESD tests; OVP functional test
Add secondary OVP on main PCB; strengthen surge immunity to IEC 61000-4-5; require CB report and factory audit
Certified PSU candidates sourced; surge test slot reserved
Prefer off-the-shelf certified adapters to reduce compliance burden
Ensure no audible coil whine in sleep mode
Provide simple controls with minimal light in sleep mode
Sleep mode not reducing brightness or sound
User disturbed during sleep; returns; negative reviews
Firmware bug; ambient light sensor miscalibration; default settings reset
SW requirements; code review; brightness limits; non-volatile settings storage
UI regression testing; photometric measurement; user acceptance tests
Add hard cap on sleep luminance; add long-press sleep toggle; add silent mode for beeps; store last state
Sleep UI spec drafted; test cases being written
Define sleep luminance target (cd/m²) and verify in dark room
Ensure compliance with accessibility and clear labeling
Seal / gasket between filter and housing
Maintain airtight sealing to prevent bypass and rattles
Air bypass; noise increase; reduced CADR
Wrong elastomer; high temperature; long-term creep; chemical exposure from cleaners
Material spec (EPDM/silicone); compression range; aging tests; assembly torque control
Accelerated aging test; periodic CADR check; visual inspection of gasket fit
Switch to silicone foam with low compression set; add mechanical stop to control compression; add cleaning chemical compatibility requirement
Candidate gasket materials requested; aging protocol drafted
Gasket design should be reusable across SKUs to reduce variants
Avoid odor emission from gasket material (VOC)
Maintain stable operation under EU EMC environment
EMI causes resets or erratic fan control
Unexpected shutdown; noise bursts; user safety concern if overheating not managed
Poor grounding; inadequate filtering; ESD strike; radiated susceptibility
PCB layout rules; shielding; TVS on I/O; proper grounding; watchdog timer
EMC pre-scan; IEC 61000-4-2 ESD test; firmware fault injection
Add common-mode chokes on PSU input; improve ground plane stitching; enhance FW brownout handling; add ESD protection to buttons
Document EMC design guidelines for reuse in next generation
Ensure compliance with EN 55014/EN 61000 series as applicable
Keep components within safe temperatures at all modes
Overtemperature of motor driver or PSU
Reduced life; shutdown; potential burn hazard at touch surfaces
Insufficient airflow over heatsinks; high ambient; dust buildup; undersized heatsink
Thermal simulation; component derating; thermal pads; overtemp sensor/NTC; auto derate
Thermal chamber tests; IR scan; firmware logs; touch temperature test
Add thermal cutoff and derating curve; improve heatsink contact; add dust-tolerant cooling path; define max touch temp per EN 60335
Thermal model created; chamber test planned
Thermal derate must not create audible step changes in sleep mode
Provide accurate air quality feedback and auto mode control
Misleading AQ display; wrong fan speed; poor perceived performance
Dust accumulation; VOC sensor aging; humidity effects; calibration loss
Sensor placement away from outlet; inlet labyrinth; periodic auto-zero routine; filter on sensor port
Self-diagnostics plausibility; field calibration check via service mode; incoming calibration verification
Add replaceable sensor cover; implement drift detection and service warning; tighten calibration process and storage conditions
Auto-zero algorithm under review; sensor cover concept drafted
Avoid making regulatory-grade claims; state accuracy and limits clearly
Sensor noise must not cause frequent speed oscillations
Excessive noise or tonal whine in sleep
Sleep disturbance; failure to meet requirement; returns
Blade pass frequency tone; motor commutation noise; housing resonance; loose parts
Fan blade optimization; motor FOC tuning; damping foam; fastener torque spec
Anechoic noise test; vibration measurement; end-of-line listening check sampling
Add resonant frequency shift ribs; implement spread-spectrum PWM; add rubber isolators; define EOL noise limit test with statistical control
FOC tuning ongoing; isolator samples ordered
Define test setup per ISO 3744/3746 and bedroom background noise assumptions
Minimize airflow reduction while reducing noise
Allow safe, tool-less filter replacement while preventing access to moving parts
Finger access to fan; filter unseats; noise; reduced CADR; safety noncompliance
Weak latch; wear; user drops unit; vibration loosens; tolerance issues
Dual-latch design; door-open interlock switch; vibration-resistant features; life-cycle latch test
Interlock functional test; door pull test; endurance cycling; safety probe test
Add redundant interlock (magnetic + switch); increase latch engagement; add door-open immediate fan stop and UI warning
Interlock switch selected; latch redesign in progress
Verify per EN 60335 accessibility and mechanical strength requirements
Ensure interlock does not create false trips from EMC/ESD
